note: Authoritative references that ground every Verifyd policy check, trust-score component, and lattice gate. Citations are stable identifiers (DOI, ISO number, regulation article, RFC, NIST publication ID) so receipts remain verifiable independent of any vendor.

NIST.AI.RMF.1.0.title: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0)
NIST.AI.RMF.1.0.publisher: U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology

NIST.AI.600-1.title: AI Risk Management Framework: Generative AI Profile
NIST.AI.600-1.publisher: U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology

key_articles.Art.13: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
key_articles.Art.50: Transparency obligations for certain AI systems

ISO.IEC.42001.2023.title: Information technology — Artificial intelligence — Management system

ISO.IEC.23894.2023.title: Information technology — Artificial intelligence — Guidance on risk management

ISO.IEC.27001.2022.title: Information security, cybersecurity and privacy protection — Information security management systems — Requirements

OECD.AI.Principles.2019.title: OECD AI Principles (Recommendation of the Council on Artificial Intelligence)

FIPS.180-4.title: Secure Hash Standard (SHS)
FIPS.180-4.publisher: U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology

RFC.6962.title: Certificate Transparency
RFC.6962.note: Append-only Merkle log model that informs Verifyd's audit chain.

OWASP.LLM.2025.title: OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications (2025)

FAIR.Principles.2016.title: The FAIR Guiding Principles for scientific data management and stewardship

FTC.AI.2023.title: Keep your AI claims in check (FTC business guidance)

Reg.B.ECOA.1003.title: Regulation B (Equal Credit Opportunity Act), adverse-action notice and reasons

no_coercion.principle: AI outputs that direct human action must not use imperative pressure absent informed-disclosure framing.

transparency.principle: AI outputs must declare source provenance and a confidence value to be auditable.

grounds.locator: Art. 13 (instructions for use, including expected accuracy and limitations)
F (Findable) and A (Accessible) — applied to source anchors

audit_trail.principle: Every ratified output must carry a hash-bound trail of session, content, and time-of-production fields.

grounds.locator: GOVERN 1.7 (mechanisms exist to track and document decisions)
SHA-256 used for content_hash and session_hash
Canonical JSON serialization for stable hashing

independent_oversight.principle: Ratification of any record requires two distinct parties; no single sovereign may unilaterally seal.

grounds.locator: Art. 14 (human oversight, including separation of duties)

agent_history.measures: Reputation signal for the AI agent identity.

promotion_ladder.principle: Status promotions follow gated transitions; forbidden shortcuts are protocol-rejected.

deposited_requires_external_anchor.principle: DEPOSITED status requires an external resolvable anchor (DOI, IPFS, or URL with span hash).

ratified_requires_quorum.principle: RATIFIED requires >=4/7 distinct Oversight Board attestations.

grounds.locator: Art. 14 (human oversight by multiple parties for high-risk systems)

no_sole_sovereign.principle: Single-party ratification is a hard protocol failure (tier_0_sovereign_forbidden).

grounds.locator: Art. 14(2) (oversight measures shall enable a natural person to override)

valf_1.principle: VALF-1 receipts are SHA-256 over canonical JSON with append-only chaining.
